title: Writing Directions
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences giving sequential travel instructions using the Te-form and identifying locations using relative spatial nouns.
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences based on the English prompts. Focus on the correct particles for movement (wo for passing through/turning AT, ni for direction).

contentItems.text: Remember: 'Turn left at the corner' uses 'wo' for the corner (where the turn happens) and 'ni' for the left (the direction).

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) How do I get to the station? 2) Please go straight. 3) Please turn left at the corner. 4) The bank is behind the hotel.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate questions with 'dou yatte', instructions with '~te kudasai', and locations with 'A no B'.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: えきへ どうやって いきますか。まっすぐ いってください。かどを ひだりに まがってください。ぎんこうは ホテルの うしろに あります。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Eki e dou yatte ikimasu ka. Massugu itte kudasai. Kado wo hidari ni magatte kudasai. Ginkou wa hoteru no ushiro ni arimasu.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: How do I get to the station? Please go straight. Please turn left at the corner. The bank is behind the hotel.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect particle usage (kado wo hidari ni) and correct spatial construction (hoteru no ushiro).

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'dou yatte ikimasu ka'?
Did the learner write 'massugu itte kudasai'?
Did the learner write 'kado wo hidari ni magatte kudasai'?
Did the learner write 'hoteru no ushiro ni arimasu'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: えきへ どうやって いきますか。まっすぐ いってください。かどを ひだりに まがってください。ぎんこうは ホテルの うしろに あります。
acceptableAlternates: えきに どうやって いきますか。まっすぐ いってください。かどを ひだりに まがってください。ぎんこうは ホテルの うしろに あります。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of directional particles and Te-form requests.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! Your directions are clear and grammatically perfect.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the particles for turning. You turn AT a place (wo) TO a direction (ni). Kado wo hidari ni.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't write 'Hoteru no ushiro desu'. Because a bank is an inanimate object, you must use 'arimasu' for its location.

hintText: Use 'wo' for the corner (where you turn) and 'ni' for the left (where you head toward).
commonMistakes: Writing 'Kado ni hidari wo' (reversing the particles).
